concerns.text: Tasks T032, T033, T035 are deferred/blocked, and T038 documents the removal of FR-006, FR-007, FR-009, FR-010. However, Constitution Principle VI (Temporal Autocorrelation Consideration) and Principle VII (Coverage Probability Reporting) are marked NON-NEGOTIABLE and explicitly mandate analyzing the UCI dataset and reporting coverage as a function of phi for real data. The tasks list fails to provide any path to satisfy these constitutional mandates, effectively weakening the project's core constraints by accepting a synthetic-only scope without a formal constitutional amendment.

Tasks T019 and T022 implement McNemar's test, explicitly deviating from Spec FR-005 which mandates a 'two-proportion z-test'. While the Plan justifies this deviation for statistical validity, the tasks do not include a mechanism to formally update the Spec or Constitution to reflect this change. This creates a semantic weakening where the implementation satisfies the Plan but violates the written Spec requirement (FR-005) without a traceable requirement change record, violating the 'Single Source of Truth' principle.

Tasks T037 and T039 (Reproducibility Verification) are marked as incomplete (checkbox unchecked) in the 'Sequential Verification Chain'. Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) is NON-NEGOTIABLE and requires that 'Every result... MUST be reproducible'. Leaving the verification tasks incomplete means the project cannot demonstrate compliance with a non-negotiable constitutional constraint, constituting a failure to preserve the requirement for verified reproducibility.
